knot_assessments.notes: Mishra's knot technique used - single extracorporeal sliding knot rather than traditional intracorporeal throws

confidence_rationale: Video shows extracorporeal knot tying technique (Mishra's knot) rather than traditional FLS intracorporeal suturing. Cannot assess suture placement accuracy, drain penetration, or gap closure as no target tissue/drain visible. Timing assessment reliable but technique assessment limited by non-standard approach.
cannot_determine: Suture placement through tissue marks - no target visible
Drain penetration accuracy - no Penrose drain visible
Gap closure quality - no slit visible
Exact suture deviation penalties - no reference marks visible
Whether this represents proper FLS Task 5 setup vs demonstration video
technique_summary: Demonstrates proficient extracorporeal sliding knot (Mishra's knot) technique with clean execution. However, this appears to be a knot tying demonstration rather than complete FLS Task 5 intracorporeal suturing with tissue penetration.
improvement_suggestions: Video does not show standard FLS Task 5 components (needle loading, tissue penetration, intracorporeal throws)
For actual FLS assessment, would need to demonstrate suture placement through marked targets and traditional surgeon's knot sequence
